license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.3rrgm56
title: CTAB DNA Extraction for genotyping
author: Yaowu Yuan

steps_raw.instruction: Grind fresh plant tissue with liquid nitrogen or silica-gel dried tissue in a 1.5 ml Eppie tube. A little silica gel grains in the tube actually helps the grinding.
Add 750 µL, CTAB DNA Extraction buffer .
Incubate the CTAB/plant extract mixture for 00:15:00 at 55 °C in the heat block and invert to mix throughout the 15 minutes.
Add 500 µL, Chloroform : IsoAmyl Alcohol (24:1) in the hood and mix the solution by invertingthe tubes ( do not vortex ).
Transfer the upper aqueous phase only to a new eppie tube (~ 500 µL ).
Add 50 µL, 7.5M Ammonium acetate followed by 500 µL, ice cold 100% ethanol and invert to mix.
Put tubes in -20 °C freezer for 00:30:00 (or longer) to precipitate the DNA.
Centrifuge at for 00:15:00 . You should see a pellet at the bottom (align the tubesso that you know where the pellet is in case you can’t see it very well).
Remove the supernatant and wash the DNA pellet as follows. (1/2)
Remove the supernatant and wash the DNA pellet as follows. (2/2)
Remove all the supernatant and allow the DNA pellet to dry in the hood (approx. 00:20:00 ). Do not over dry the pellet since it will be hard to re-dissolve.
Resuspend the DNA in 100 µL, dH2O .
Run the DNA on a 1.0% agarose gel to check the quality of the DNA.
